step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with agent1's assessment. Performing a frenectomy prior to addressing a diastema ensures that there will be no interference with the newly available space post-surgery, allowing for better control over tooth positioning through subsequent orthodontic treatments. Additionally, waiting until after the canines have erupted might complicate achieving ideal spacing and alignment outcomes as the permanent molars play crucial roles in overall arch development and stability.
